_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A1/001 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 001 · Yesterday at the Park

npc.text: Hi, Anna. What did you do yesterday?

routes.meaning: Anna says she went to the park with her mom.
examples: I went to the park with my mom.
I went to the park with mom yesterday.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I went to the park with my mom.”

routes.meaning: Anna went to the park with someone else.
examples: I went to the park with my dad.
I went to the park with my friend.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I went to the park with my mom.”

partial.message: Tell Tom where you went: “I went to the park with my mom.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked about yesterday. Say “I went to the park with my mom.”

npc.text: That sounds fun! What did you do there?

routes.meaning: Anna played with her ball and ran on the grass.
examples: I played with my ball and I ran on the grass.
I played with my ball and ran.
I played ball and ran on the grass.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I played with my ball and I ran on the grass.”
💬 Try saying: “I played with my ball and I ran on the grass.”

partial.message: Tell Tom what you did: “I played with my ball and I ran on the grass.”

off_topic.message: Tom wants to know what you did. Say “I played with my ball and I ran on the grass.”

npc.text: Did you climb the big tree?

routes.meaning: Anna did not climb the tree because it was too high.
examples: No, I didn't, because it was too high.
No, I did not climb it.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “No, I didn't, because it was too high.”

routes.meaning: Anna says she did climb the tree.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “No, I didn't, because it was too high.”

partial.message: Did you climb the tree? Say “No, I didn't, because it was too high.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked about the big tree. Try “No, I didn't, because it was too high.”

partial.message: Tell Tom about it: “That was fun!”

off_topic.message: Say how it was: “That was fun!”

npc.text: I went to the park too, but it started to rain.

routes.meaning: Anna reacts and asks what Tom did then.
examples: Oh no! So what did you do then?
Oh no! What did you do?
So what did you do then?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Oh no! So what did you do then?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks if Tom got wet.
examples: Oh no! Did you get wet?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Oh no! So what did you do then?”

partial.message: React to Tom: “Oh no! So what did you do then?”

off_topic.message: It started to rain! Say “Oh no! So what did you do then?”

npc.text: Yes, a little bit! I ran home fast.

routes.meaning: Anna asks what Tom did then.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What did you do then?”

partial.message: Ask Tom: “What did you do then?”

off_topic.message: Tom ran home. Ask “What did you do then?”

npc.text: I went home and watched TV.

learning_objective: Make a plan with a friend

routes.meaning: Anna suggests playing together next time.
examples: Next time we can play together.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Next time, let's play together!”

examples: What did you see on TV?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Next time, let's play together!”

partial.message: Make a plan: “Next time, let's play together!”

off_topic.message: Tom watched TV. Say “Next time, let's play together!”

npc.text: I watched a fun cartoon! Next time, let's play together. Okay?

routes.meaning: Anna agrees to play together next time.

off_topic.message: Tom wants to play next time. Say “Yes, let's play together!”

npc.text: Yay! It's a plan. See you at the park, Anna!
